hypothesis: Multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization that uses fast MD/GCMC simulations as low-fidelity evaluations and long-timescale MD simulations as high-fidelity evaluations will exhibit surrogate model representation sensitivity that is inversely correlated with fidelity level—specifically, polymer-specific pretrained embeddings (PolyNC, polyBERT) will provide a statistically significant advantage over general chemical space embeddings in the low-fidelity surrogate but will show diminishing differential advantage in the high-fidelity surrogate, because the additional structural information encoded in polymer-specific representations primarily compensates for the conformational sampling limitations inherent to fast simulations rather than capturing intrinsic chemical affinity differences that are fully resolved by long-timescale dynamics.
